commentaries.author: Clement Of Alexandria
commentaries.content: What, I ask, more graceful, more gay-coloured, than flowers? What, I say, more delightful than lilies or roses? "And if God so clothe the grass, which is to-day in the field, and to morrow is cast into the oven, how much more will He clothe you, O ye of little faith!"

commentaries.author: Tertullian of Carthage
commentaries.content:
And as an example of clothing we have the lilies.
Will appear a little further on. Meanwhile, how is it that He chides them as being "of little faith? "
